title: Align capability truth matrix and documentation
outOfScope: Implementing runtime parity in plan Work Item 3
Changing installer or upgrade runtime behavior

sources.reason: Structured capability status source of truth.
Human-readable capability matrix and evidence links.
Existing capability status and documentation regression coverage.
Documentation claim and release metadata scanning boundary.
User-authorized serial plan, Work Item 2 boundary, and evidence requirements.

rawUserRequest: 按评审结论执行计划，严格串行完成工单；建立唯一 Capability Matrix，修正 README、Installation、Upgrade、Calibration、Lifecycle、CLI Reference 和多语言文档中把未来设计写成当前能力的内容。

declaredIntent.summary: 建立 capability truth matrix 并让所有文档声明引用状态、命令、源文件、测试和 release evidence；未实现能力统一标为 planned。

riskAssessment.reason: The Work Item changes public capability claims but does not change runtime behavior or publish a release.

executionDecision.reason: User authorized serial plan execution and this is the next Work Item after the preceding root correction closed.

preReviewWarnings: Do not claim runtime behavior from template-only files.
Every status must link command, source, test, and release evidence.
Do not consume Work Item 3 runtime scope.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Every matrix capability has one of implemented, template_only, adopter_installed, or planned status with evidence links.
README and installation/upgrade/calibration/lifecycle documents do not present planned capabilities as implemented.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record checkpoints to prevent scope drift into Work Item 3.

acceptance: The capability matrix is the unique status source and every capability uses the four required status enum values, with command, source, test, and release evidence links.
repositoryRole and template/adopter boundaries are accurate and no default template fact is presented as adopter runtime capability.
Ten-stage Calibration, Candidate Activation, Wizard, Ownership Manifest, Disable, and Uninstall are explicitly planned unless evidence proves an allowed status.
README, installation, upgrade, calibration, lifecycle, CLI-reference, and Japanese/Chinese documentation claims are aligned with the matrix.
Regression scanning fails on unsupported current-capability claims and passes the corrected documentation; runtime parity remains out of scope.

The functional correction passes make check-ai-contract, make check-ai-scope, ruff format --check, make check-ai-change-summary, and the complete archive/commit/PR/merge/ai-close-work-item lifecycle; no budget-only Work Item is created.
guidelines: Documentation claims must cite structured evidence.
Template and adopter repositories must be distinguished.
Planned behavior must not be described as implemented.
Do not alter runtime scope belonging to Work Item 3.

intent.problem: Review found documentation that describes future calibration, activation, wizard, ownership, and lifecycle behavior as if it were current, and repository classification defaults that can mislead adopters.
constraints: No runtime implementation in this Work Item.
Preserve historical plans and Work Item evidence.
Keep the next Work Item serial and separate.
intent.rationale: A structured capability truth source prevents documentation drift and makes future readiness claims evidence-backed.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User authorized functional capability-truth correction and its bounded archive budget reservation.

rollbackNote: Revert documentation and matrix changes; preserve historical Work Item records.

repaymentRecords.reason: Functional capability-truth correction; no budget-only Work Item. Repay during final plan cleanup.
Full Finish measured 40048 lines after adding the functional claim-enforcement gate; reserve bounded headroom in this Work Item, not a budget-only Work Item, and repay during final plan cleanup.
